# Ingredients


 \normalsize

- 1 pound pasta (e.g., fettuccine or bucatini)
- 1 pound chicken breast.
- 6 tablespoons butter
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1.5 cups heavy cream
- 3/4 cup parmesan cheese
- 1 zucchini
- 1 shallot
- 1/8 tsp chili flakes
- 4 garlic cloves
- 5 oz spinach
- 1 tsp gochujang
- 3 tsp mayonnaise
- 2/3 cup kimchi
- 4 slices of bread
- 2/3 cup cheddar cheese
- 4 tablespoons everything seasoning.
- 2 cans of baked beans
- 1 can of black beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of white kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of red kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 large yellow onion
- 1 pound of ground beef
- 1 pound of bacon
- 16 ounces of BBQ sauce
- 1 egg
- 2 1/2 tablespoons ice water
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 10 tablespoons butter
- 7 eggs
- 1/2 cups parmesan cheese
- 1 cup swiss and gruyere shredded cheese blend
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon italian seasoning
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/8 teaspoon chili flakes
- 1 tsp dried basil (or 4-5 fresh basil leaves)
- 12 ounces cherry tomatoes

 \normalsize


# Recipe One



 \normalsize

![Alfredo Pasta (Serves 4)](website/images/Alfredo.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize



 \normalsize

- 1 pound pasta (e.g., fettuccine or bucatini)
- 1 pound chicken breast.
- 6 tablespoons butter
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1.5 cups heavy cream
- 3/4 cup parmesan cheese
- 1 zucchini
- 1 shallot

 \normalsize


 \normalsize

1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees.
1. Season chicken with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Cook on rimmed baking sheet until cooked 15-25 minutes.
1. Mince garlic, cut zucchini into half moons, and dice shallot.
1. Melt butter in a large frying pan. Add garlic, cook until just starting to brown (1-2 minutes).
1. Add zucchini to the pan and cook until tender.
1. Add heavy cream to pan and simmer for about ten minutes.
1. Add in parmesan cheese and stir until smooth. Remove from heat.
1. When ready, boil pasta according to package instructions. Save a cup of pasta water.
1. Mix pasta and sauce. If needed, add pasta water to loosen the sauce.
1. Serve with grated Parmesan.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Two


 \normalsize

![Kimchi Grilled Cheese](website/images/KimchiGrilledCheese.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize



 \normalsize

- 1/8 tsp chili flakes
- 4 garlic cloves
- 5 oz spinach
- 1 tsp gochujang
- 3 tsp mayonnaise
- 2/3 cup kimchi
- 4 slices of bread
- 2/3 cup cheddar cheese
- 4 tablespoons everything seasoning.

 \normalsize


 \normalsize

1. In a frying pan, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil with chili flakes.
1. Add spinach and cook until wilted.
1. Mix gochujang and mayonnaise in a small bowl and spread on one side of each slice of bread. Sprinkle with everything seasoning and press it into the bread.
1. Build sandwiches by layering spinach, kimchi, and cheese between two slices of bread.
1. Fry in butter until golden brown on both sides.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Three


 \normalsize

![BBQ Chili](website/images/BBQChili.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize



 \normalsize

- 2 cans of baked beans
- 1 can of black beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of white kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of red kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 large yellow onion
- 1 pound of ground beef
- 1 pound of bacon
- 16 ounces of BBQ sauce

 \normalsize


 \normalsize

1. Dice the yellow onion.
1. Drain and rinse black, white, and red beans.
1. Crumble and cook beef in a frying pan.
1. Cook bacon and cut into bite size pieces.
1. Combine all ingredients in a crock pot. Cook at low heat for 6-7 hours. Stir occasionally.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Breakfast Recipe



 \normalsize

![Garden Tomato Quiche (Serves 6-8)](website/images/GardenTomatoQuiche.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize



 \normalsize

- 1 egg
- 2 1/2 tablespoons ice water
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 10 tablespoons butter
- 7 eggs
- 1/2 cups parmesan cheese
- 1 cup swiss and gruyere shredded cheese blend
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon italian seasoning
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/8 teaspoon chili flakes
- 1 tsp dried basil (or 4-5 fresh basil leaves)
- 12 ounces cherry tomatoes

 \normalsize


 \normalsize

1. Grease a pie plate.
1. Whisk egg and ice water in a small bowl.
1. Cut butter into small cubes and mix into flour and salt. If you have a food processor, use it. Otherwise mix with a fork until it is a sand-like mixture.
1. Add the egg mixture into the bowl with the flour and mix until a dough forms.
1. Roll out dough to fit a pie plate, pierce with a fork, and press it into the pan.
1. Put the pie plate in the freezer while the oven preheats to 375 degrees.
1. Blind bake quiche crust with pie weights (can use dried beans instead) for 15 minutes.
1. Mix 3 egg yolks, 4 whole eggs, parmesan cheese, shredded cheese, milk, italian seasoning, salt, chili flakes, and basil.
1. Halve cherry tomatoes.
1. Pour egg mixture into the pie plate.
1. Place cherry tomatoes cut-side up, filling the surface area of the quiche. Sprinkle with salt and italian seasoning.
1. Bake for 35-50 minutes, until the edges are set but the center has a slight jiggle or it reaches an internal temperature near 175.

 \normalsize
